texto: Every man on earth is sick with the fever of sin, with the blindness of sin and is overcome with its fury. As sins consist mostly of malice and pride, it is necessary to treat everyone who suffers from the malady of sin with kindness and love. This is an important truth, which we often forget. Very often we act in the opposite manner: we add malice to malice by our anger, we oppose pride with pride. Thus, evil grows within us and does not decrease; it is not cured – rather it spreads
explicacao: Citação de John of Kronstadt sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Every man on earth is sick with the fever of sin, with the blindness of sin and is overcome with …” articula reflexao, anger, conquering-evil em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como conquering-evil e evil.

texto: Do not fear the conflict, do not flee it. Where there is no struggle, there is no virtue; where faith and love are not tempted, it is not possible to be sure whether they are really present. They are proved and revealed in adversity, that is, in difficult and grievous circumstances, both outward and inward - during sickness, sorrow, or privations
explicacao: Citação de John of Kronstadt sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Do not fear the conflict, do not flee it. Where there is no struggle, there is no virtue; where f…” articula superacao, conflict, faith em registro de superacao, com relevância para contextos como faith.

frase_original: Do not fear the conflict, do not flee it. Where there is no struggle, there is no virtue; where faith and love are not tempted, it is not possible to be sure whether they are really present. They are proved and revealed in adversity, that is, in difficult and grievous circumstances, both outward and inward - during sickness, sorrow, or privations

texto: Do not be irritated either with those who sin or those who offend; do not have a passion for noticing every sin in your neighbour, and for judging him, as we are in the habit of doing. Everyone shall give an answer to God for himself. Everyone has a conscience; everyone hears God's Word, and knows God's Will either from books or from conversation with other people. Especially do not look with evil intention upon the sins of your elders, which do not regard you; "to his own master he standeth or falleth." Correct your own sins, amend your own life.
explicacao: Citação de John of Kronstadt sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Do not be irritated either with those who sin or those who offend; do not have a passion for noti…” articula desenvolvimento-pessoal, judgment-of-others, sin em registro de desenvolvimento pessoal, com relevância para contextos como termino e sin.

Especially do not look with evil intention upon the sins of your elders, which do not regard you; "to his own master he standeth or falleth." Correct your own sins, amend your own life.
